title: ICE Agent Castro Arrested in Texas After Fleeing Minnesota Charges
excerpt: ICE agent Christian Castro, charged with four counts of second-degree assault and filing a false police report for a January 14 shooting in Minneapolis, was apprehended in Harlingen, Texas, by the Texas Rangers after Minnesota Bureau of Criminal Apprehension agents tracked him there. DHS called the arrest a 'political stunt,' while Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison said it showed no one is above the law.
content:
On May 29, 2026, ICE agent Christian J. Castro, 52, was arrested at a home in Harlingen, Texas, by Texas Rangers who executed a nationwide arrest warrant issued by the Hennepin County Attorney's Office.

  • Castro had been charged two weeks earlier with four counts of second-degree assault with a dangerous weapon and one misdemeanor count of falsely reporting a crime.
  • The charges stemmed from a January 14, 2026, incident during Operation Metro Surge in which Castro fired through the front door of a home, striking Venezuelan national Julio Sosa-Celis in the leg. Video evidence contradicted Castro's account that he acted in self-defense after being attacked with a shovel.
  • Federal prosecutors had initially charged Sosa-Celis with assault but dropped the charges after acknowledging that the officers involved had made untruthful statements.
  • DHS called Castro's arrest "unlawful" and a "political stunt," stating the matter should be handled by federal authorities.
  • Castro was booked into Cameron County Jail in Texas and denied bond, pending an extradition hearing.

Castro is the second federal agent charged with state crimes in connection with Operation Metro Surge. Minnesota AG Ellison stated: "In Minnesota, we believe in equal justice under the law. That means nobody is above the law, including agents of the federal government."
mediaPlaceholder: Booking photo of Christian Castro and scene outside the Minneapolis home where the shooting occurred
